description: Storing per-request data in a module-scope global and expecting it to persist, leaking data across requests or losing it on cold start.
body: Failure mode: a handler caches request-specific data (user, cart, auth) in a module-level variable, assuming the instance persists; warm reuse then serves one user's data to another, and cold starts lose it entirely. Detection signals: intermittent cross-user data bleed; behavior differs between first and subsequent requests; state vanishes unpredictably. Do not keep per-request state in shared module scope. Safer fallback: store durable or per-request state externally (database/cache), reserve module globals for read-only config and connection reuse, and test with each request on a fresh instance.
